name: auto_31_aviation_right
premise: An aircraft on a multi-leg route triggers intermittent engine vibration warnings that do not reproduce consistently on the ground. Ops Manager Colin Park can cancel the next flight and order a borescope inspection that will strand passengers overnight and disrupt crews, or he can swap to a spare aircraft that has a minor deferred cabin system defect and is not configured for wheelchair seating. The airport has limited gate capacity and weather delays are compounding. A viral video accuses the airline of cutting corners after a previous incident. Colin must decide quickly before crew duty limits expire. External scrutiny from regulators and community observers has intensified because the outcome could redefine expectations in Aviation Safety.

agents.name: Ops Manager Colin Park
agents.identity: I am the airline operations manager responsible for passenger safety decisions when schedule pressure conflicts with conservative maintenance margins.
agents.goal: I want to prevent an avoidable incident without creating a policy that strands vulnerable travelers and triggers cascading disruption.

agents.name: Maintenance Chief Sara Ali
agents.identity: I am the maintenance chief accountable for airworthiness decisions when sensor warnings are ambiguous and inspections consume scarce hangar time.
agents.goal: I want decisions that prioritize verified safety signals over reputational and financial pressure to keep aircraft flying.

choices: I will cancel the flight and order the full inspection, accepting disruption and costs to prioritize verified mechanical safety and rebuild trust.
I will dispatch a spare aircraft with accommodations managed on the ground and schedule the inspection at the destination, accepting some risk to avoid stranding passengers and cascading delays.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Information is incomplete and the consequences are immediate. Ops Manager Colin Park must choose between two defensible actions with different risks. What does Ops Manager Colin Park do?
